company.name: Genspark
company.tagline: Two product launches, one ruthless pivot, and $200M+ ARR in 11 months
company.summary: Genspark inverts the standard SaaS launch pattern. There is no latent period — Day 0 was a $60M pre-revenue seed and a TechCrunch headline, engineered around founder Eric Jing's reputation as a former Baidu VP and Xiaodu CEO with deep China-AI roots (early Microsoft Xiaoice technical work, founding Bing Asia search engineer). The company spent nine months as an AI search engine, then in April 2025 abandoned 5M Sparkpages users and launched Super Agent three weeks behind Manus's viral wave. Nine days later it had $10M ARR — the fastest in AI product history.

events.title: MainFunc Inc founded
events.description: Eric Jing (former Baidu VP, Xiaodu CEO, early Microsoft Xiaoice technical lead and Bing Asia engineer since 2006) and Kay Zhu (former Xiaodu CTO, ex-Google ranking) incorporate MainFunc, Inc. in Palo Alto. Wen Sang (MIT PhD, Smarking founder, YC alum) joins as COO.

events.title: Day 1: Seed $60M + Sparkpages launch
events.description: Pre-revenue $60M seed at $260M post-money, led by Lanchi Ventures. Genspark and the Sparkpages product launch the same day. TechCrunch and SiliconANGLE break the news in parallel — funding + product + founder pedigree fired together as a single narrative event.

events.title: Autopilot Agent ships
events.description: Asynchronous multi-step research agent — Genspark's first agentic product, packaged as 'a smarter Sparkpage.' Important in retrospect: the team was already prototyping the agent direction six months before the Super Agent pivot.

events.title: 5M+ cumulative users
events.description: Sparkpages crosses 5M registered users on the AI search story. Free product, almost no monetization signal in the public record. The denominator that would later be sacrificed.

events.title: Series A $100M / $530M
events.description: SiliconANGLE breaks the Series A — $100M at $530M post-money. 5.3× valuation jump in eight months on a still-thin revenue base. The AI search narrative is at peak credibility just weeks before the pivot.

events.title: Super Agent launch
events.description: Mixture-of-Agents architecture: 8–9 LLMs, 80+ tools, 10+ proprietary datasets. Claims GAIA benchmark 87.8% (Manus: 86%). Killer demo: 'AI Call For Me' — the agent dials real phones. Three weeks behind Manus's viral wave, Genspark launches the comparable product with the missing feature.

events.title: VentureBeat: 'ups the ante'
events.description: VentureBeat publishes a deep dive positioning Super Agent as the Western counter to Manus. The framing is the win: every subsequent piece treats Genspark and Manus as a paired story.

events.title: $10M ARR in 9 days
events.description: Nine days after launch, Super Agent crosses $10M ARR — the fastest AI product to that line on record. The number itself becomes the next narrative beat.

events.title: Japan 'AI quit my job' goes viral
events.description: Japanese users use 'AI Call For Me' to phone in their resignations. The screenshots and audio clips spread on X and YouTube in Japanese tech-Twitter. Japan becomes Genspark's #1 paid market within months.

events.title: AI Slides ships
events.description: Document/PDF-to-presentation with built-in research. First in the rapid-fire 'workspace primitive' release cadence that bolts new agentic verbs onto the Super Agent base.

events.title: AI Sheets ships
events.description: One-prompt spreadsheet generation. Second workspace primitive in a month.

events.title: $36M ARR / 2M users / 20 people
events.description: Forty-five days after Super Agent: $36M ARR, 2M+ users, 20-person team. The number triple — '20 people, 45 days, $36M ARR' — becomes Wen Sang's headline across DealMakers, ProductLed, Founders Coho, and Evolving Edge podcasts.

events.title: $50M ARR / enterprise pricing
events.description: Five months in: $50M ARR. Enterprise tier rolls out at $30/seat/month, planting the seed for the AI Workspace push.

events.title: Genspark AI Browser
events.description: Native browser with 169 on-device open-source models — full local privacy. Lands at #9 on Product Hunt. Functions as a reposition demo: Genspark is no longer just a chat interface.

events.title: Bloomberg: unicorn + LG/SBI
events.description: Bloomberg breaks the unicorn news nine days early. LG Technology Ventures and SBI Investment join the Series B — Korean and Japanese strategic capital staked into a US-headquartered Chinese-founded company.

events.title: Series B $275M + AI Workspace + Microsoft
events.description: Three announcements, one day. Series B $275M at $1.25B (Emergence Capital lead). AI Workspace launches. Microsoft Agent 365 names Genspark a launch partner. The single highest-density media day of the entire arc.

events.title: Microsoft Agent 365 launch partner
events.description: Genspark integrates into Microsoft 365 (Outlook, Teams) at Microsoft Ignite, on the day Microsoft's Agent 365 platform debuts. Genspark's own Series B announcement two days later (Nov 20) re-amplifies the partnership.

events.title: Sparkpages publicly retired
events.description: Genspark openly redirects the old Sparkpages surface into the Workspace experience. 5M registered users on the original product written off in public — most companies hide pivots; Genspark made it the story.

events.title: Series B extension to $300M / $100M ARR
events.description: Existing investors top up the Series B by $25M to $300M. AI Workspace 2.0 ships the same day. BusinessWire headline: 'crosses $100M ARR in 9 months,' with 1,000+ enterprise organizations.

events.title: AI Workspace 2.0
events.description: Same-day product release alongside the funding extension. 1,000+ enterprise customers cited in the press release — the AI Workspace narrative compounds with the funding event.

events.title: Series B ext $385M / $1.6B + AI Workspace 3.0 + Claw debut
events.description: Bundled announcement: Series B tops up to $385M total at $1.6B (Mirae Asset, HartBeat, Markham Valley, Keisuke Honda join). AI Workspace 3.0 launches alongside Genspark Claw — the company's first 'AI Employee'. $200M+ run rate cited the same day. Total disclosed funding crosses $545M.

events.title: $200M+ run rate
events.description: Eleven months from Super Agent launch. Disclosed alongside the $385M close — funding and revenue milestones bundled, again.

events.title: AI Workspace 4.0 + Claw Desktop / Office
events.description: Workspace 4.0 with Genspark Claw for Desktop and native Microsoft Office integration. Enterprise-readiness signal — extends Claw from web to local files four weeks after the Workspace 3.0 + Claw debut.

events.title: Eric Jing rejoins X
events.description: Eric Jing's @ericjing_ai account, dormant since November 2023, becomes active around the Super Agent launch. The 'Seeing AGI' essay series begins. Founder IP gets activated exactly when the second product needs a face.

events.title: OpenAI case study published
events.description: OpenAI publishes an official case study on Genspark's use of GPT-4.1 and Realtime API. Vendor-as-validator: when your model supplier writes the marketing for you.

platforms.name: X (Twitter)
platforms.description: X is where the Super Agent vs Manus comparison lived. @genspark_ai's launch tweet pulled 317K+ views in three weeks; @ericjing_ai's account, dormant from late 2023, came back to life exactly when the agent narrative needed a founder face. Wen Sang (@sang_wen) carries the long-form discourse.
platforms.catalyst: April 2, 2025 — @genspark_ai's Super Agent thread cross-posts with VentureBeat coverage and triggers the wave of 'Genspark vs Manus' creator demos that ran through April.
platforms.whenItWorks: When you have a comparable, demo-able feature your category leader is missing — a 'wait, you can do that?' moment a creator can record in 60 seconds
platforms.whenItDoesnt: If your founders aren't posting in their own voice. Genspark's catch was reactivating Eric Jing on X around the pivot — without that, the company account is talking to itself

platforms.name: YouTube
platforms.description: Genspark's growth on YouTube was almost entirely earned — Julian Goldie, AI commentary channels, and Japanese tech YouTubers cut 'Genspark vs Manus' demos in the weeks after launch. The official Genspark channel exists but is secondary; the creator demos are the asset.
platforms.catalyst: Mid-April 2025 — third-party 'FREE Manus alternative' demo videos start landing 100K+ views each. The 'AI Call For Me' phone-dialing scene becomes the screenshot of the era.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the 'before/after' on a single user task is dramatic enough that a creator can show it in two minutes — phone calls, slide generation, browser automation
platforms.whenItDoesnt: For abstract enterprise products. YouTube rewards the visible demo; if the magic is invisible (workflow, retention) the format fails

platforms.name: Hacker News
platforms.description: HN is structurally cooler on Genspark than on most dev-tool launches. Threads on Sparkpages, Super Agent, and AI Workspace surface but with lower comment volume and the recurring 'thin wrapper' skepticism. Genspark's growth did not come through HN — it came around HN.
platforms.catalyst: No single catalyst. The most-cited HN thread is the late 'What is Genspark?' discussion in February 2026 — a sign that Genspark broke out without HN being the first to know.
platforms.whenItWorks: When the technical claim is verifiable on launch day — open-source on-device browser models, a real benchmark number with a reproducible setup
platforms.whenItDoesnt: When the product is a closed-loop demo. HN punishes 'trust me' framing; Genspark's GAIA 87.8% claim is a textbook example of what HN will sandbag

platforms.name: Reddit
platforms.description: r/genspark_ai exists but is more support forum than evangelism hub. Heavy threads on credit-billing complaints and refund disputes — the cost of growing user count faster than the support org. Reddit is not where Genspark's narrative gets made; it's where post-purchase friction surfaces.
platforms.catalyst: No single moment. Slow-burn community emergence post-Super Agent. The signal value is negative: when Reddit complaint volume spiked, Genspark's actual product issues surfaced there before customer-success could catch them.
platforms.whenItWorks: As an early-warning system for product friction. Reddit threads predict the issues your support tickets are about to spike on
platforms.whenItDoesnt: As an acquisition channel for AI agents in 2025–26. The Reddit user base is more skeptical than Twitter's; expect a tougher reception

platforms.name: LinkedIn
platforms.description: Higher-leverage for Genspark than for most AI startups — because the AI Workspace pitch is enterprise from day one. Wen Sang's LinkedIn carries the long-form 'how we hit $200M ARR' content that doesn't fit on X. Eric Jing's funding announcements get re-shared by Microsoft, LG, and SBI executives, which is the actual enterprise channel.
platforms.catalyst: Series B announcement (November 20, 2025) — Microsoft's enterprise leadership amplifies the Agent 365 partnership. That single re-share is more valuable for enterprise pipeline than any campaign.
platforms.whenItWorks: When you have a strategic enterprise partner whose own executives will re-share your news. LG, SBI, and Microsoft did the work for Genspark
platforms.whenItDoesnt: Without that strategic amplification. A Genspark-only post on LinkedIn would have decayed within hours. The leverage is in the partner's audience, not yours

platforms.name: Instagram + TikTok
platforms.description: Unlike most US-first dev-tool startups that correctly skip Instagram, Genspark runs a dedicated @genspark_japan account (~2.6K followers) and a TikTok presence around 'Claw Party' offline events. The reason: Japan is Genspark's largest paid subscription market, and Japanese consumer-AI discovery is more visual-first than the US dev-Twitter equivalent.
platforms.catalyst: Spring 2025 — Japanese users sharing 'AI Call For Me' clips of resignation calls. The narrative moves from text-based screenshots on X to vertical video on TikTok and Instagram Reels.
platforms.whenItWorks: For a product with a visible, emotional consumer use case in a visual-first market. Genspark's Japanese resignation-call use case is exactly that
platforms.whenItDoesnt: For US-first developer tools. The Genspark playbook here is regional — skipping Instagram is the right call for a US dev-tool product
